pattern: Each query only changes one character, so re-deriving the answer from scratch every time wastes the fact that most of the string's run structure survives the edit untouched.
hints: After an update, only the run containing the changed index (and possibly its neighbors) can change -- everything else keeps its previous run length.
Track the boundaries between runs of the same character, along with each run's length, and keep the running maximum available for fast lookup after every query.

When a character changes, remove it from its old run (splitting that run around it if it was in the middle), then check whether it now matches its new left and right neighbors so their runs can merge into a single longer run.

edge_inputs.description: single character string, single no-op query
splitting a uniform run by changing its middle character
merging two runs into one by bridging a differing middle character
repeated queries on the same index toggling the character back and forth
a query that sets a character to the value it already has
several queries touching different regions of a mixed string
a query at the very last position of the string

stress.description: a moderately long string over a tiny alphabet with an equal number of queries, so long runs form and repeatedly split and merge
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 2500))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(n))
    qc = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(n))
    qi = [rng.randrange(n) for _ in range(n)]
    return [s, qc, qi]
